title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: The government has recently launched a new health campaign.
questions.secondSentence: A new health campaign (1) _________ by the government.

questions.firstSentence: She found it difficult to understand the lecturer's accent.
I would prefer you not to smoke in the house.
solutions: would ('d) rather you did not smoke

questions.firstSentence: It was wrong of you to borrow my car without asking.
The town council is considering a new traffic management scheme.
questions.secondSentence: A new traffic management scheme (1) _________ by the town council.

questions.firstSentence: If I had known you were coming, I would have cooked more food.
questions.secondSentence: (1) _________ you were coming, I would have cooked more food.
